briefdescription.para: This is the Error handler callback function and this function increments the the error counter so that the main thread knows the number of errors.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference to the AxiEthernet device instance.

briefdescription.para: This is the Fifo handler function and will increment a shared counter that can be tested by the main thread of operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference to the Fifo instance.

briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates the usage usage of the Axi Ethernet by sending and receiving frames in interrupt driven fifo mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Intc component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Axi Ethernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the AXIFIFO component.
is Device ID of the Axi Ethernet Device , typically XPAR_<AXIETHERNET_instance>_DEVICE_ID value from xparameters.h.
is the Interrupt ID and is typically XPAR_<INTC_instance>_<AXIETHERNET_instance>_VEC_ID value from xparameters.h.
is the Interrupt ID and is typically XPAR_<INTC_instance>_<AXIETHERNET_instance>_VEC_ID value from xparameters.h.
is the interrupt id for fifo.

simplesect.para: AxiFifo hardware must be initialized before initializing AxiEthernet. Since AxiFifo reset line is connected to the AxiEthernet reset line, a reset of AxiFifo hardware during its initialization would reset AxiEthernet.

briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates the usage of the Axi Ethernet by sending and receiving a single frame in interrupt mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Fifo component.

briefdescription.para: This example sends a packet from non-contiguous memory locations.

detaileddescription.para: The header is stored in one area. The payload data is calculated and written to the packet FIFO one byte at a time.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Fifo component.

briefdescription.para: This example sends and receives a batch of frames.

detaileddescription.para: Frame reception is handled in this function and not in the callback function.
Use this method of reception when interrupt latency is important.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Fifo component.

briefdescription.para: This is the Receive handler callback function for examples 1 and 2.

detaileddescription.para: It will increment a shared counter, receive and validate the frame.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference to the Fifo device instance.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the device but preserves the options set by the user.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Fifo component.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS to indicate success.
XST_FAILURE.to indicate failure.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for the Axi Ethernet example.

detaileddescription.para: This function is not included if the example is generated from the TestAppGen test tool.

briefdescription.para: This is the Error handler callback function and this function increments the the error counter so that the main thread knows the number of errors.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference to the AxiEthernet device instance.
is a bitmask of the pending interrupts.

briefdescription.para: Implements examples that utilize the Axi Ethernet's interrupt driven FIFO direct packet transfer mode to send and receive frames.

listitem.para: How to perform simple send and receive.
AxiEthernetSingleFrameIntrExample() demonstrates the simplest way to send and receive frames in interrupt driven FIFO direct mode.
AxiEthernetSingleFrameNonContIntrExample demonstrates how to handle frames that are stored in more than one memory location.
AxiEthernetMultipleFramesIntrExample demonstrates how to defer frame reception so that CPU intensive receive functions are not performed in interrupt context.
AxiEthernetErrorHandler() demonstrates how to manage asynchronous errors.
AxiEthernetResetDevice() demonstrates how to reset the driver/HW while maintaining driver/HW state.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a asa 4/30/10 First release based on the ll temac driver 3.00a bss 10/22/12 Added support for Fast Interrupt Handlers. 3.01a srt 02/14/13 Added support for Zynq (CR 681136) 3.02a srt 08/06/13 Fixed CR 727634 - Modified FifoHandler() logic to reflect the bit changes in the Interrupt Status Register as per the latest AXI FIFO stream IP. 5.4 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. ms 04/05/17 Added return tags in functions for proper documentation while generating doxygen. 5.6 adk 03/07/17 Fixed CR#979023 Example failed to compile. 5.12 sk 27/04/21 Fix multiple definition errors reported when peripheral tests are generated.
